mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or out-of-sample classification accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion regimes, or the signal reverses direction without regime filtering in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with contracting real GDP indicates a synchronized economic downturn, which systematically reduces corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when both series move in the specified direction, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside falling real personal income confirms weakening aggregate demand, forcing S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next week during contractionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances, or the regression slope remains statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 24-month window.
